items.title: While it makes sense for the US, Iran and Russia to keep oil prices high, it makes no sense to have inflation ruin most others and in fact hurt the US...
items.description_html:
While it makes sense for the US, Iran and Russia to keep oil prices high, it makes no sense to have inflation ruin most others and in fact hurt the US.

I believe we will see lower prices on crude, and a return to availability soon. It makes sense for a longer term policy response... Like to change fpi taxation, to introduce duties on end use goods, to push local manufacturing, refining of materials etc, and to explore local crude oil resources.

But it doesn't make sense to unnecessarily hike rates. The govt has done an amazing job managing shortages but it's time to build the next India. Semiconductors, machinery, cold storage, and so on.

Rbis work with the rupee right now is actually good, and remember that if it buys dollars on the way in, it must sell them on the way out. It's not selling enough, and doesn't have the insti linkages that say boj or boe has, but in general the response has been decent. We still run a surplus on liquidity. They should top this up with a distribution of their own profits properly, regardless of what our govt needs.

I would stay prepared for short term pain but it does look like peak pessimism on India. I just don't think a country of smart hungry people will just wiffle away all the economic growth because some uncles decide there's nothing here. We grow because we are ambitious, sometimes despite our govt and the bureaucracy, and the next decade is going to be good here. Two years ago it was just india and no one else. Today's everyone else but India. Both will prove to be wrong.

Re Distrust of civic institutions compounds the pre-existing generalized distrust in our hyper-diverse society, leaving India with a severe social capital deficit.

We buy gold to cover this deficit.

Gold is imported and paid for in foreign currency, and hence shows up on the deficit side of the current account.

As CapitalMind CEO @deepakshenoy showed, if gold imports are excluded, India’s current account would have been in surplus for 11 of the past 12 quarters.

In other words, our social capital deficit is exacerbating macroeconomic problems during an approaching crisis.

items.title: RT ANI: #WATCH | Mumbai: Chief Economist, CareEdge Ratings, Rajani Sinha says, “India's forex reserves are falling mainly because of weak capital flo...
items.description_html:
RT ANI
#WATCH | Mumbai: Chief Economist, CareEdge Ratings, Rajani Sinha says, “India's forex reserves are falling mainly because of weak capital flows into the country. Because of this, we are seeing a sharp weakening of the currency, and the RBI has been intervening in the forex market to control the depreciation of the rupee. That is why, since the start of the West Asia crisis, we have seen our forex reserves deplete by around 27 to 28 billion dollars. When I say capital flows, not only are we seeing high FII outflows since the West Asia crisis started—so for March and April, cumulatively we have seen FII outflows of around $20 billion—but if we look at the whole of the last fiscal year, we also saw FII outflows of around $16 billion or so…”

The scale of the reduction in Chinese oil imports is increasingly difficult to explain with available data.

On paper, Beijing is currently running its economy with ~1/4 less oil than pre-war. But demand destruction (or shift in petchem feedstock) can hardly explain *all* of it. items.title: The UK government is spinning its waiver on imports of Russian refined oil products as "temporary" (and briefing reporters it will be reviewed in 3 mo...
items.description_html:
The UK government is spinning its waiver on imports of Russian refined oil products as "temporary" (and briefing reporters it will be reviewed in 3 months).

But the legal text doesn't say that ⤵️. It says the waiver is of "indefinite duration." Of course, as any other waiver, it can be rescinded. But, if so, the legal text says the gov "will endevour" to provide the market with a 4-month notice. So, using the briefing guidance, that's 3 months for a review + 4 months notice = end of 2026.

Agree with the gov briefing in one aspect: cock-up.

items.title: In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY. Exports to China ...

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.title: BREAKING: Foreign holdings of US Treasuries fell -$139 billion in March, to $9.35 trillion, the largest monthly decline since September 2022. Japan, t...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Foreign holdings of US Treasuries fell -$139 billion in March, to $9.35 trillion, the largest monthly decline since September 2022.

Japan, the largest foreign holder, reduced its stockpile by -$48 billion, to $1.19 trillion, to their lowest since December 2025, as the Bank of Japan sold US Treasuries to fund yen intervention.

China, the 3rd-largest holder, trimmed its holdings by -$41 billion, to $652 billion, their lowest level since September 2008.

China’s holdings have now declined -$109 billion, or -14%, since the start of 2025.

By comparison, the UK, the 2nd-largest holder, added +$30 billion, with total holdings rising to a record $927 billion.

US Treasury markets are becoming even more volatile.

items.title: Technology stocks are driving historic market gains: The Information Technology sector has returned +225.7% since the bull market started on October 1...
items.description_html:
Technology stocks are driving historic market gains:

The Information Technology sector has returned +225.7% since the bull market started on October 12th, 2022, leading every other sector.

This is followed by Communication Services, which has surged +212.3% over the same period.

Both sectors have outperformed the S&P 500's gain of +107.0% by more than 100 percentage points.

By comparison, industrials, consumer discretionary, and financials have risen +102.1%, +76.6%, and +70.8%, respectively.

Therefore, just 2 of 11 sectors have outperformed the broader market during the current bull run.

The market cannot thrive without tech.

items.title: China's chip exports are surging: China's chip exports jumped +100% YoY in April, to a record ~$31 billion. This figure has TRIPLED over the last 2 ye...
items.description_html:
China's chip exports are surging:

China's chip exports jumped +100% YoY in April, to a record ~$31 billion.

This figure has TRIPLED over the last 2 years.

At the same time, overseas sales of laptops, tablets, and their components jumped +47% YoY.

Goldman Sachs and Nomura estimate that semiconductors, computers, and other AI-related products accounted for ~50% of China's export growth last month.

In total, Chinese exports rose +14% YoY in April, to $359 billion, the highest monthly reading on record.

Put differently, Chinese companies were generating ~$500 million in export revenue every hour on average last month.

China's economy is evolving with AI.

items.title: China's real estate market continues to plummet: New home prices in 70 Chinese cities fell -3.5% YoY in April, with 49 cities reporting monthly declin...
items.description_html:
China's real estate market continues to plummet:

New home prices in 70 Chinese cities fell -3.5% YoY in April, with 49 cities reporting monthly declines.

Tier-one cities, including Shanghai and Shenzhen, recorded a +0.1% MoM gain.

By comparison, tier-two and tier-three cities continued to struggle, falling -0.1% and -0.3% MoM, respectively, pointing to an uneven recovery.

Meanwhile, property investment fell -13.7% in the first 4 months of 2026, exceeding the -11.2% contraction posted in Q1.

Overall, persistent oversupply is likely to weigh on prices for another 1 to 2 years before a broader recovery can take place.

China’s 2008 moment continues.

items.title: US oil inventories are collapsing: Total US crude inventories, including the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR), fell by -17.8 million barrels last wee...
items.description_html:
US oil inventories are collapsing:

Total US crude inventories, including the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR), fell by -17.8 million barrels last week, the largest weekly drawdown in history.

Commercial crude inventories fell -7.9 million barrels, the biggest decline since mid-February.

Furthermore, the SPR alone shed -9.9 million barrels, the largest weekly drain on record.

This marks the 8th consecutive weekly decline, the longest streak in 3 years.

Since the Iran War began, 42 million barrels, or 10% of the entire US SPR, have been drained, mostly via exports to Asia.

The SPR now stands at 374 million barrels, the lowest since July 2024, leaving the US with a significantly reduced buffer against future supply shocks.

The US emergency oil cushion is disappearing faster than at any point in history.

items.description_html:

1. Immediate and comprehensive ceasefire on all fronts

2. All parties mutually agree to refrain from targeting infrastructure

3. Freedom of navigation in the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz is guaranteed under a "joint monitoring mechanism"

5. Negotiations on outstanding issues will begin within a maximum of 7 days

6. Iran's President Pezeshkian is leading efforts to restrain the IRGC from overstepping on political and diplomatic policy-making

The agreement is expected to be announced within the next "few hours."

Turn on our post notifications @KobeissiLetter, we will be bringing it to you first.

Pakistani officials are reportedly brokering the deal.

items.title: Investor demand for downside protection is plummeting: The average 3-month put-to-call skew of S&P 500 single stocks is down to 0.04, the 4th-lowest r...
items.description_html:
Investor demand for downside protection is plummeting:

The average 3-month put-to-call skew of S&P 500 single stocks is down to 0.04, the 4th-lowest reading over the last 20 years.

This measures how much more investors are paying for downside protection via put options than for upside exposure through call options.

The lower the reading, the less investors are paying to protect themselves against a market decline.

The average 3-month put-to-call skew has fallen -75% since March, posting the sharpest drop since April-May 2025.

This metric is even lower than during the 2021 meme stock frenzy.

Investors are no longer thinking about downside risk.

items.title: Global investors are cashing out of South Korean stocks: Foreign investors have sold -$22 billion of South Korean stocks so far in May, on track for t...
items.description_html:
Global investors are cashing out of South Korean stocks:

Foreign investors have sold -$22 billion of South Korean stocks so far in May, on track for the largest monthly outflow on record.

Year-to-date, overseas investors have offloaded ~$60 billion in KOSPI stocks, on pace for the largest annual sale on record.

Most of the sales have been concentrated in Samsung and SK Hynix stocks, with ~$20 billion sold in SK Hynix alone year-to-date, including ~$12 billion so far in May.

Since November 2025, foreign investor outflows in SK Hynix have totaled ~$26.7 billion.

Global investors are one of the largest owners of South Korean equities, accounting for ~39% of the total KOSPI index market cap.

Foreign capital is exiting the world's best-performing market at an unprecedented pace.

items.title: BREAKING: The Trump Administration is investing $2 billion in quantum computing companies and will receive equity stakes in return, per WSJ. Details i...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The Trump Administration is investing $2 billion in quantum computing companies and will receive equity stakes in return, per WSJ.

Details include:

1. $1 billion of the package will be awarded to IBM, $IBM

2. Chip maker GlobalFoundries, $GFS, is receiving $375 million in funding

3. The rest of the companies will receive $100 million each, except for startup Diraq, which is slated to get $38 million

4. Multiple other public companies will receive funds including D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion

Trump's next big bet is on quantum computing.

items.title: It's official. SpaceX is going straight to retail investors. "Certain of the shares of Class A common stock offered hereby will, at our request, be of...
items.description_html:
It's official.

SpaceX is going straight to retail investors.

"Certain of the shares of Class A common stock offered hereby will, at our request, be offered to retail investors," SpaceX says in their S-1 filing today.

These shares will be available through Charles Schwab, Fidelity, Robinhood, SoFi Securities, and ETRADE.

Retail will have a huge role in this historic IPO.

items.title: China's economy is losing momentum: Fixed-asset investment in China fell -1.6% YoY in the first 4 months of 2026, slipping back into contraction terri...
items.description_html:
China's economy is losing momentum:

Fixed-asset investment in China fell -1.6% YoY in the first 4 months of 2026, slipping back into contraction territory.

The decline was driven by a -13.7% year-to-date drop in property development, building on an -11.2% decline in the first 3 months of 2026.

Furthermore, industrial production rose just +4.1% YoY in April, the weakest reading since mid-2023.

At the same time, retail sales grew just +0.2% YoY, the worst reading since China's economy reopened after the pandemic in December 2022.

This comes as car sales dropped -15% YoY, the steepest decline since mid-2022.

Meanwhile, gold, silver, and jewelry sales collapsed -21% YoY, reversing sharply from the buying frenzy seen earlier this year when surging precious metal prices drove speculative demand.

China's economic slowdown is broadening.

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: CITI On Axis Bank Buy, TP Rs 1620 Management meet takeaways [1] West Asia crisis – No visible deterioration and remains comfortable o...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
CITI On Axis Bank
Buy, TP Rs 1620
Management meet takeaways
[1] West Asia crisis – No visible deterioration and remains comfortable on growth and asset quality; likely carries sufficient buffer to absorb most adverse scenario.
[2] Disciplined growth strategy: To outperform system average growth; critically growth will not come at the cost of risk discipline.
[3] Committed to through-the-cycle NIM of 3.8% given structural levers; we expect some near-term NIM pressure from the mix shift.
[4] ECL transition impact on networth to be marginal; structurally marginal increase in credit cost (sector-wide dynamic).
[5] Strategic deposit franchise deepening underway; comfortable operating at ~92% LDR +/- 2-3%.
[6] Likely to deliver positive operating leverage (on C/I basis) in FY27 and cost to assets will be optimized over medium term

CITI on ICICI BK
Buy, TP Rs 1720
Management meet Key takeaways
[1] Broad-based growth momentum led by step-up in retail (mortgages, PL, GL); corporate growth supported by a structural shift in funding from bond markets to banks; continued momentum in business banking.
[2] NIMs range-bound with mix optimization the lever, while CoD may remain elevated.
[3] West Asia crisis shows no visible portfolio stress; actively monitoring borrowers exposed to global trade and supply chain disruptions; tighter credit filters on new borrower onboarding in vulnerable sectors.
[4] Fee income trajectory likely to improve toward double-digit growth; opex discipline maintained with growth expected to be below revenue/asset growth.
[5] ECL transition - given existing buffers, no networth impact anticipated; credit cost to marginally rise on a flow basis.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: RT CMO Maharashtra: 🤝CM Devendra Fadnavis presided over the signing and exchange of MoUs between the Government of Maharashtra (Energy Department) ...
items.description_html:
RT CMO Maharashtra
🤝CM Devendra Fadnavis presided over the signing and exchange of MoUs between the Government of Maharashtra (Energy Department) and various companies in Mantralaya, Mumbai, today.

Details of the MOUs:

🔸Adani Power Limited and its Coastal-Maha Atomic Energy Limited

For setting up a nuclear power plant at Barsu, Dist. Ratnagiri.
✅Proposed Capacity: 6000 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹1,50,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 4500 + 7500 = 12,000

🔸Reliance Industries Ltd.

For setting up Bharat Small Reactors/Small Modular Reactor (SMR)/Nuclear Power Reactors at Purnagadh, Dist. Ratnagiri.
✅Proposed Capacity: 2 X 220 MW to 6 X 1200 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹8000 to ₹2,00,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 3000 + 1,00,000 = 1,03,000

🔸NTPC (National Thermal Power Corporation) Ltd.

For setting up a nuclear power plant at Devgad, Dist. Sindhudurg.
✅Proposed Capacity: 6 Units/ 4000 MW - 7200 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹1,00,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 5000

Minister Atul Save, Business Head, Nuclear Energy at Adani Group, Vivek Sharma, President Nuclear Energy Business Reliance Industries Ltd., Ranjay Sharan, CEO, NTPC, Arnada Prasad Samal and other senior officials were present.

🤝 मुख्यमंत्री देवेंद्र फडणवीस यांच्या प्रमुख उपस्थितीत आज मंत्रालय, मुंबई येथे महाराष्ट्र शासन (ऊर्जा विभाग) आणि विविध कंपन्यांमध्ये सामंजस्य करार करण्यात आले.

सामंजस्य करारांचे तपशील

🔸अदानी पॉवर लिमिटेड व त्यांची कोस्टल-महा ॲटोमिक एनर्जी लिमिटेड

बारसू, जि. रत्नागिरी येथे अणुऊर्जा प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता - 6000 मेगावॅट
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक - ₹1,50,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 4500 + 7500 = 12,000

🔸 रिलायन्स इंडस्ट्रीज लिमिटेड

पूर्णगड जि. रत्नागिरी येथे 'भारत स्मॉल रिॲक्टर्स' / स्मॉल मॉड्युलर रिॲक्टर (SMR)/ अणुऊर्जा रिॲक्टर प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता : 2 X 220 MW ते 6 X 1200 MW
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक : ₹8000 ते ₹2,00,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 3000 + 1,00,000 = 1,03,000

🔸 नॅशनल थर्मल पॉवर कॉर्पोरेशन लिमिटेड (NTPC)

सिंधुदुर्ग जिल्ह्यात देवगड येथे अणुऊर्जा प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता : एकूण 6 युनिट्सद्वारे 4000 ते 7200 मेगावॅट
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक : ₹1,00,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 5000

यावेळी मंत्री अतुल सावे, अदानी समूहाच्या न्यूक्लिअर एनर्जीचे बिझनेस हेड विवेक शर्मा, रिलायन्स इंडस्ट्रीज लिमिटेडच्या न्यूक्लिअर एनर्जी बिझनेसचे अध्यक्ष, रंजय शरण, एनटीपीसीचे मुख्य कार्यकारी अधिकारी अर्णदा प्रसाद सामल आणि वरिष्ठ अधिकारी उपस्थित होते.

items.title: Labour shortages we have seen across industrial segments , from auto to PEB to chemicals as well , some main factors such as elections , summer and fe...
items.description_html:
Labour shortages we have seen across industrial segments , from auto to PEB to chemicals as well , some main factors such as elections , summer and few more smaller regional factors

But the more important thing is lack of motive to work due to the across political parties freebies culture where if one can earn 40% of monthly salary by sitting at home in the form of freebies , then get free ration from the government and also other schemes for unemployment incentives , the incentive to work reduces drastically and we are seeing this slippery slope of lack of productivity only increase with all parties encouraging freebies !


ishmohit: Spoke to few managements in last 2-3 months

Huge issue that is emerging is labour shortage. People expect reward without hardwork. Wrong type of incentives being given in our society

items.title: Nurture Well crossed ₹1,000 Cr revenue this year Turning into full-fledged FMCG brand Key numbers: • EBITDA grew 30%+ to ~₹90 Cr, Revenue grew by 3...
items.description_html:
Nurture Well crossed ₹1,000 Cr revenue this year Turning into full-fledged FMCG brand

Key numbers: • EBITDA grew 30%+ to ~₹90 Cr, Revenue grew by 34%
• PAT rose 86% to ~₹100 Cr
• Distribution expanded in North India
• Exports entered new geographies
• Capacity expansion is underway

The interesting part is the category.

Biscuits and affordable snacks are everyday consumption products. That makes demand relatively more resilient, even when costs and logistics become uncertain.

For Nurture Well, the next phase depends on execution — scaling capacity, managing supply chain pressure, and converting export opportunities into sustainable growth.

items.title: Interesting divergence playing out globally Markets linked to the AI trade are massively outperforming AI linked Markets: South Korea, Taiwan, Japan D...
items.description_html:
Interesting divergence playing out globally

Markets linked to the AI trade are massively outperforming

AI linked Markets: South Korea, Taiwan, Japan

Domestic Growth / “Anti-AI” Markets: India, Indonesia, Philippines

Why this divergence?

Korea & Taiwan are directly benefiting from the AI infrastructure boom

Samsung & SK Hynix are riding the HBM memory cycle

TSMC is at the center of AI chip manufacturing

Japan is benefiting through semiconductor equipment & exporters

This is becoming a clear split globally:

AI Asia = Korea, Taiwan & Japan

Domestic Growth Asia = India, Indonesia & Philippines

Interesting part is that even within markets, the rally is becoming extremely concentrated

A handful of AI linked companies are driving entire indices higher

items.title: Stylam Industries - Q4FY26 update🟩 The key update from the latest concall is that the new laminates plant is now close to commercial production The...
items.description_html:
Stylam Industries - Q4FY26 update🟩

The key update from the latest concall is that the new laminates plant is now close to commercial production

There was some delay mainly due to environment clearance requirements

Management indicated that the new plant will contribute 250-300 Cr REV in FY27

So, the direction remains positive, at peak utilisation it is expected to generate around 900-1,000 Cr REV

By FY28, it can potentially generate 600-700 Cr REV, assuming 60-70% utilisation

Demand commentary remains strong

Management mentioned that April was the best month in the company’s history, with sales crossing 100 Cr from the existing plant itself

In the Q4FY26 concall, management sounded more conservative & guided for around 20-25% growth over the medium term

However, considering April sales momentum, new plant contribution & operating leverage, there is room for positive surprise

Export market remains the key growth driver

Margin outlook remains strong

Management indicated that the new plant can operate at 22-24% margin profile

The important point is that most fixed costs, manpower cost & initial expenses of the new plant are already built into the current cost base

So, once the new plant starts contributing REV, operating leverage should flow through

A great story worth tracking, Markets already rewarding the company in anticipation of a bumper FY27.


Equity Insights Elite: Stylam Industries - Key insights from the latest concall

Company gearing up for strong growth in FY27 as new capacity comes online

Management is targeting FY27 REV of 1500-1600 Cr

(Targeting ⏫40%+ Growth, TTM REV: 1112 Cr)

New laminates plant expected to contribute 300-400

items.title: PEB / Prefab Sector - Q4FY26 Key Insights Now let’s discuss each company in detail, one by one - 1️⃣ Interarch Building 2️⃣ EPACK Prefab 3️⃣ M&...
items.description_html:
PEB / Prefab Sector - Q4FY26 Key Insights

Now let’s discuss each company in detail, one by one -

1️⃣ Interarch Building
2️⃣ EPACK Prefab
3️⃣ M&B Engineering

Interarch Building : FY26 highlights
REV - 1,898 Cr 🔼31%
EBITDA - 176 Cr 🔼29%
PAT - 135 Cr 🔼25%

Management Commentary & Future Outlook
- Targeting REV : 2,200 Cr/2,500 Cr by FY27/FY28
- Order book stood at 1,703 Cr as of April'26
- 127 Cr spent in Capex, expanding capacities in Andhra Pradesh, Kiccha & Gujarat
- Andhra Pradesh & Gujarat plants are expected to be commissioned by Q2FY27
- Andhra Pradesh expansion is focused on heavy steel structures
- Gujarat PEB plant is located in a major industrial cluster & targets sectors like EVs and semiconductors
- Company secured export certifications for Canada and USA
- Export orders worth over 40 Cr were received in FY26
- MoU signed with ER Steel for structural steel & PEB business across Canada and North America
- Total REV potential : 3500 Cr after all capacities come live

EPACK Prefab Technologies : FY26 highlights
REV - 1,525 Cr 🔼35%
EBITDA - 160 Cr 🔼36%
PAT - 93 Cr 🔼56%

Management Commentary & Future Outlook
- FY27 REV guidance to surpass 1,900 Cr
- Management is guiding for 30% growth in the prefab division
- EBITDA margin guidance remains 10.5%
- Order book stood at 1,113 Cr as of March’26
- Prefab business grew around 45% YoY in FY26
- Capacity utilisation improved through the year & reached 83% in Q4FY26
- Operating cash flow stood at 136 Cr, around 85% of EBITDA, Net cash at 201 Cr
- Management believes 30% growth is achievable in Prefab division based on order book, pipeline & capacity additions

M&B Engineering : FY26 highlights
REV - 1,260 Cr 🔼27%
PAT - 93 Cr 🔼20%

Management Commentary & Future Outlook
- FY27 growth guidance stands at 23-25%
- Management has not given specific margin guidance due to uncertainty in steel prices, freight, FX & geopolitical conditions
- Unexecuted order book stood at 1,083 Cr, growing 35% YoY
- FY26 order inflow stood at 1,539 Cr 🔼28%
- FY26 capex was 33 Cr, while FY27 capex is expected around 100 Cr
- Phenix division FY26 REV stood at 985 Cr 🔼29%
- Proflex division FY26 REV stood at 275 Cr 🔼23%
- Company is adding around 20,000 MT capacity at Sanand, expected to be commissioned in Q2FY27
- Export REV target is around 275-300 Cr in FY27 vs 165 Cr in FY26
- Export EBITDA margins are expected at 16-17% vs domestic margins of around 10-11%
- Margin pressure in Q4 was due to FX loss, input cost inflation & freight costs

Sector continue to witness tailwinds from industrial capex, warehousing, logistics, renewable energy, data centres, semiconductors & large manufacturing projects

There is a structural shift visible from conventional construction to faster, factory made & execution efficient solutions

If you want us to cover other sectors in a similar way, do mention below. Also, let us know which sector should be covered first.

items.title: PEB sector was one of the most chased themes recently The reason was simple Sector had strong tailwinds, good order books, capacity expansion visibili...
items.description_html:
PEB sector was one of the most chased themes recently

The reason was simple

Sector had strong tailwinds, good order books, capacity expansion visibility & demand visibility from industrial capex, warehousing, logistics, data centres, renewables, EVs & manufacturing

Because of this, valuations expanded very sharply
Most were trading sub 20x in the space but once it caught market attention it even moved towards 35x earnings

At that stage, the story was good, but the valuation comfort was missing

When a sector becomes crowded, more often than not, it disappoints

Most of these companies are now trading below 20x trailing earnings & around 15-16x on fwd earnings

This changes the risk reward meaningfully.

So the real question is -
Is the PEB story over? Absolutely not.

PEB is clearly gaining share from conventional construction because it saves time, improves execution speed & gives better scalability for industrial customers. So the sector opportunity remains intact.

But is the hype over? Absolutely yes.

Recent Correction has removed a large part of the excess valuation froth

Earlier, investors were paying a premium for the same story.

Now, the same theme is available at much more reasonable valuations

When everyone starts chasing the same theme, valuations can move far ahead of earnings

At that point, even a small miss in margins, execution or guidance can lead to sharp correction

The takeaway is simple. Do not chase hype. It never ends well.

This is the biggest lesson from the PEB space.

items.title: India’s Internet economy has a long runway across multiple verticals 1. E-commerce remains one of the largest opportunities, with current GMV around ...
items.description_html:
India’s Internet economy has a long runway across multiple verticals

1. E-commerce remains one of the largest opportunities, with current GMV around $70 Bn & expected to reach $174-214 Bn by FY30.

2. B2B e-commerce looks like the biggest structural white space. Current TAM is $45 Bn with less than 2% digital penetration, but the market opportunity can scale towards $2.5 Tn by FY30.

3. Food delivery is already more consolidated, with Zomato + Swiggy holding around 85% share. Growth is still healthy at 17-23% CAGR, but the market is more mature compared now.

4. Quick commerce is the fastest growing theme. The market has grown 100% YoY from $6.2 Bn in FY25 to nearly $11-12 Bn in FY26E.

Rest mentioned⏬

The largest opportunities are in B2B e-commerce, e-commerce, quick commerce, food delivery & mobility.

src : ICICI Sec

India’s total installed capacity reached 533 GW in FY26

Earlier, the opportunity was mainly in module assembly

Now the opportunity is expanding into cells, wafers, ingots & polysilicon

The hard part is not believing in compounding. It is owning a business long enough for the arithmetic to do its work. Most investors sell early because they have no framework for holding.
I should be open about my interest. I run http://valueresearchstocks.com. We do the research and give clear buy, hold and sell calls. I own every stock we recommend, by regulation and by choice. Thirty days to read the work and decide. Full refund if it does not convince you.
Your friend is welcome.

Fun fact: Romania’s president, Nicușor Dan, was one of the 11 students to solve the legendary Problem 6 at the 1988 IMO.

He scored a perfect 42/42 that year, alongside future Fields Medalist Ngô Bảo Châu. Ravi Vakil also solved it.

Terence Tao, aged just 13, got 1/7 on that problem, aced almost everything else, and still won gold.

items.title: RT DepressedBergman: Satyajit Ray on how audience from other countries can connect with his movies: "There are obvious risks in exposing an audience t...
items.description_html:
RT DepressedBergman
Satyajit Ray on how audience from other countries can connect with his movies:

"There are obvious risks in exposing an audience to a wholly alien culture. There was an American lady, for instance, who was so upset by the spectacle of Indians eating with their fingers that she had to leave the theatre as soon as the second dinner episode commenced. But such squeamishness is not common. If details confuse, or offend, or go over the head, there is always the human element to fall back upon.

In 'Apur Sansar' (1959), Apu attends the wedding of a friend’s sister. The bridegroom (this is an arranged marriage) arrives and is found to be touched in the head. With the bride’s mother refusing to give away her daughter, the marriage seems on the verge of being abandoned. If it doesn’t take place at the appointed hour, the bride would be cursed and made unmarriageable for life. The crisis is averted at the last moment by Apu offering to step in as the groom. A Western viewer ignorant of orthodox Hindu customs must find the episode highly bizarre. But since Apu himself finds it so, and since his action is prompted by compassion, the viewer accepts it on moral grounds, though given no opportunity to weigh the pros and cons of a seemingly irrational practice.

Faced with a film from an alien culture, swamped by details which evoke no responses in him, a viewer will often find that the only aspect which offers a common ground is the moral one. Broad concepts of good and evil, right and wrong, do not vary much from culture to culture. Human behaviour also falls within patterns which are largely familiar and predictable. Abnormal situations may affect such behaviour as, say, in Hitler’s Germany; but as long as a film makes the context clear, a viewer is unlikely to lose his bearings. This can happen only when the development of the story itself turns on social and historical factors of which the viewer has no knowledge."

("Deep Focus- Reflections on Cinema", Satyajit Ray, 2011)

P.S: On this day, 67 years ago, 'Apur Sansar' was released in India.

items.description_html:
Rate hikes are not the first port of call for the RBI to defend the rupee even now, @kalrajs23 and I report.

Instead they will watch for pass through of higher oil prices into core and headline inflation to judge timing and extent of rate hikes.

Rationale:

🔸many other options remain to defend rupee, hardly any have been activated.
🔸 rate defence of rupee will need steep hikes (not one 50bps move) and that will kill growth.
🔸 Inflation still seen within tolerance band.

Read the thinking below (not just the headline!)

items.title: RT Pulse Of Profit: Jain Resource Recycling Ltd corrected nearly 40% recently amid concerns around weak operating cash flow and rising receivables. @T...
items.description_html:
RT Pulse Of Profit
Jain Resource Recycling Ltd corrected nearly 40% recently amid concerns around weak operating cash flow and rising receivables.

@Tijori1

But management says the issue is largely accounting-related, not operational 👇

Key clarification from the concall:

• Reported cash flow was impacted due to NFRA accounting requirements
• Receivables discounted with banks now need to be shown separately instead of netting off
• Management claims actual cash flows remain healthy
Other important points:
• Copper prices & volumes up 26% in recent months
• No fresh bank borrowing in the last 4 months
• Working capital funded through internal accruals
• Inventory levels already reduced from Q3 to Q4
• Targeting reduction in working capital cycle from 66 days to below 60 days
• Receivable days currently managed at 15–18 days
Management expects working capital to turn positive from Q2 onwards.

Important to watch now:
👉 Whether upcoming quarters validate management commentary through improving cash generation and lower working capital intensity.

items.description_html:
Life Insurance Corp: Guidance - - Concall Update

Management achieved its long-held ambition to cross INR 5,000 crore in premium income through Bancassurance and Alternate Channels in FY26.

Management expects persistency to improve across all cohorts in the future, driven by the increased ticket size of policies, with the full effect of changes from October 2024 yet to unfold.

The company anticipates significantly high maturity claims in FY26 and FY27, particularly from a cohort of Jeevan Jyoti policies with high sum assured that were sold 25 years ago.

The dividend payout ratio will depend on future regulatory changes, specifically the planned introduction of risk-based capital, which requires building reserves to maintain a comfortable solvency level.

Management expects VNB margins to further improve, with a goal to cross 20%, but acknowledges that the journey will be gradual and dynamic, focusing on VNB growth through improved margins or business growth.

items.description_html:
Talbros Automotive Components: Guidance - - Concall Update

Management expects to deliver 15% to 20% year-on-year revenue growth in FY27, targeting to cross INR 1,000 crores in top line for the year, while maintaining EBITDA margins between 17% to 18%.

The company plans a CapEx of INR 51 crores in FY26 and INR 103 crores in FY27, with specific allocations for the current year including INR 16 crores for the Gasket division, INR 60 crores for the Forging division, INR 20 crores for Marelli Talbros, and INR 7 crores for Talbros Marugo.

Joint ventures are projected for significant growth in the coming year, with Marelli Talbros Chassis Systems expected to grow between 35% to 40% and Talbros Marugo around 15% in top line.

Standalone businesses, Gasket and Forging, are expected to achieve double-digit top-line growth in the current year, driven by INR 50 to INR 70 crores in new billing from new part numbers, with forging commercialization starting in October.

Management anticipates the second half of FY27 to be significantly better than the first half, with Q1 FY27's top line expected to be in line with Q4 FY26.

IRB Infrastructure Developers: Guidance - - Concall Update

The company aims to scale its asset base to approximately INR1.4 trillion over the next three years.

For FY27, construction and O&M revenue is expected to comfortably exceed INR3,000 crores.

Toll revenue is projected to grow around 10% for FY27, with a target to reach a five-digit gross revenue number, and a 20% cash flow growth for the combined IRB and Private InvIT toll business.

Profit is guided to grow at a 25% CAGR, and the company is on track to achieve a net debt-zero situation within the next five years.

The company does not foresee any capital raising requirements for IRB, as growth will primarily come from asset rotation by moving assets from Private InvIT to Public InvIT and redeploying the unlocked capital.

GPT Infraprojects: Revenue Guidance - - Concall Update

Management confirmed a higher revenue guidance for FY '27, expecting 27% to 30% growth, after missing the previous year's projection.

The prior year's revenue guidance was missed due to West Bengal elections in March, which caused labor shortages and affected execution.

The FY '27 guidance includes only Q4 numbers of INR30 crores from the acquired Alcon business, as it was merged effective January 1, 2026.

Management expressed confidence in achieving the FY '27 growth, citing new contracts and labor returning to sites, with no anticipated execution risks.

The order inflow guidance for FY '27 is approximately INR3,000 crores, with management confident in achieving this target.

items.title: Sansera Engineering: Guidance - - Concall Update Management expects CapEx for FY '27 to be similar to FY '26, which was INR 5,097 million, with invest...
items.description_html:
Sansera Engineering: Guidance - - Concall Update

Management expects CapEx for FY '27 to be similar to FY '26, which was INR 5,097 million, with investments directed towards expanding ICE capabilities and new ADS facilities.

The ADS segment has a retained guidance of roughly INR 550 crores to INR 600 crores for the next year, with expectations for margins to move closer to 25-30% as the new facility reaches full utilization.

The company aims for an overall revenue of INR 8,000 crores to INR 10,000 crores by the end of the decade, with a long-term strategic target of 40% of business from non-auto, Tech Agnostic, and xEV components, and 60% from auto-ICE.

For the auto business, H1 FY '27 is expected to be relatively stronger than H2, aided by a favorable base effect.

The energy storage solution program with a global OEM is expected to generate annual revenue between INR 80 crore to INR 100 crore.

items.title: Bosch: Guidance - - Concall Update Bosch Limited's outlook for fiscal year 2027 is one of "cautious optimism," anticipating a "flattish" overall growt...
items.description_html:
Bosch: Guidance - - Concall Update

Bosch Limited's outlook for fiscal year 2027 is one of "cautious optimism," anticipating a "flattish" overall growth due to potential headwinds like the West Asia crisis impacting crude oil prices, but confident in the government's capital expenditure to bolster domestic demand.

The Indian automotive industry is expected to see steady growth in FY '27, with specific segment projections: Passenger Vehicles (steady growth, but Q1 demand may be affected by geopolitical uncertainties), Commercial Vehicles (stable growth), and Tractors (stable growth, with El Nino as a downside risk).

The three-wheeler segment is likely to experience positive growth, while the two-wheeler segment expects healthy growth, though a below-normal monsoon could weigh on rural demand.

Content per vehicle is expected to constantly increase in the upcoming fiscal year, particularly with the upcoming CAFE Phase 3 regulations scheduled for April 2027, which will meaningfully improve content per vehicle.

The joint venture with Brakes India Private Limited and Wheels India Limited for advanced air systems is expected to commence operations by the end of 2026, with samples in 2027 and series readiness by 2028.

items.title: Even after 250bps rate hikes from May’22, the economy saw a surge in leveraged consumption led by pent-up demand… Our Understanding Retail credit co...
items.description_html:
Even after 250bps rate hikes from May’22, the economy saw a surge in leveraged consumption led by pent-up demand…

Our Understanding

Retail credit compounded near 19 percent through Dec 25 while disposable income per capita grew just 9.2 percent, so a widening share of consumption is funded by borrowing rather than earnings. The signal stays benign only while repayment capacity and asset quality hold across the cycle.

Lenders with retail exposure capture the volume tailwind, and demand flows through to autos, durables and discretionary categories. The cost sits in rising household leverage, where unsecured books are most sensitive once credit costs normalise. Net, growth is real but durability rests on income.

For allocators the texture matters more than the headline, since secured pools like gold and home loans behave differently from personal loans and cards when stress arrives. The 40 percent gold loan print blends real demand with gold price, so its credit signal reads softer than it looks.


Exencial Research Partners: http://x.com/i/article/2057343123660099584

items.title: In past instances of stress, value retail, including grocery and jewellery, has outperformed Our Understanding Across Indian retail the same ordering ...
items.description_html:
In past instances of stress, value retail, including grocery and jewellery, has outperformed

Our Understanding

Across Indian retail the same ordering repeats whenever growth softens. Households trim apparel and eating out well before they cut food or gold. FY21 is the clean test, with apparel SSG collapsing toward minus fifty percent while DMart and Titan jewellery fell only modestly.

The resilient end gains relative footing. Grocery and jewellery sustained positive or shallow SSG through the worst quarters and rebounded fastest. Discretionary apparel and QSR lose first and deepest, with Pantaloons and Shoppers Stop both near minus fifty in FY21. Net, the spread widens under stress.

For allocators the read is a relative resilience map rather than a timing call. As FY27E growth settles near six percent, the historical pattern points to value retail holding SSG better than discretionary formats. The signal breaks if apparel SSG stops decelerating relative to grocery and jewellery.


Exencial Research Partners: http://x.com/i/article/2057343123660099584

items.title: Pre-Market Update!! Billions in motion - from Byculla to Riyadh, Wall Street to Taiwan - India's deal engine roars alongside global titans. • India a...
items.description_html:
Pre-Market Update!!

Billions in motion - from Byculla to Riyadh, Wall Street to Taiwan - India's deal engine roars alongside global titans.

• India and Italy have set an ambitious target of reaching €20 billion in bilateral trade by 2029, signalling a deepened economic partnership between the two nations aimed at boosting commerce and investment flows.

• RBI - The Reserve Bank of India has arranged a $5 billion currency swap facility to inject liquidity into the financial system, addressing tightening monetary conditions and ensuring smoother functioning of the domestic credit markets.

• Blackstone - Global investment giant Blackstone has pledged its shareholding in Mphasis as collateral to mobilise $700 million in fresh capital, underscoring the firm's confidence in the IT company's long-term valuation prospects.

• Sammaan Capital has formally joined Abu Dhabi's IHC Group after the conglomerate concluded a landmark $1 billion (₹8,850 crore) strategic investment among India's largest-ever FDI deals in financial services securing a 28.5% equity stake.

• CloudExtel - Macquarie-backed CloudExtel plans to deploy ₹1,500–2,000 crore over three to five years, acquiring smaller cable operators and niche players to capture a larger slice of the rapidly expanding network-as-a-service (NaaS) market.

• Varun Beverages has extended its exclusive bottling and trademark licence pact with PepsiCo India by a decade, pushing the agreement's tenure to April 2049 and cementing a long-term strategic alliance between the two beverage partners.

• AurionPro has bagged one of its most significant US market mandates to date a $33 million fintech engagement marking a major breakthrough as the company deepens its footprint in the North American financial technology landscape.

• Bikaji Foods has greenlit an additional investment of up to $5 million into its wholly-owned New Jersey-based subsidiary, Bikaji Foods International USA Corp, reinforcing its strategic push to expand the brand's reach across American markets.

• Kesar India's wholly-owned subsidiary, Kesar Infrastructures, has secured a ₹160 crore EPC contract for a residential development at Siddharth Nagar, Byculla, marking a significant win in Mumbai's competitive construction sector.

• Man Industries has wrapped up its ₹1,000 crore acquisition in Saudi Arabia, with its subsidiary MISIC completing the full buyout of National Pipe Company, strengthening its manufacturing foothold in the Gulf region.

• Laurus Labs - Pharmaceutical major Laurus Labs has earned a coveted spot in the Dow Jones Best-in-Class Indices, a prestigious global benchmark - a recognition that reflects the company's robust sustainability and corporate governance credentials.

• Maruti Suzuki will raise prices across its entire vehicle portfolio by up to ₹30,000 effective June 2026, citing relentless inflationary headwinds and persistently elevated input costs as the primary drivers behind the revision.

• VA Wabag has secured a "medium-value" (₹100–250 crore) design, build, and operation contract for a state-of-the-art wastewater treatment facility in Delhi, adding another municipal infrastructure win to its growing order pipeline.

• AMD has committed $10 billion to build AI chips in Taiwan, a sweeping investment aimed squarely at narrowing the competitive gap with Nvidia and staking a stronger claim in the booming artificial intelligence semiconductor arena.

items.title: The naysayers have been comparing the current bull market to that of the late 1990s. Our comparison of the two shows fewer excesses in the current bul...
items.description_html:
The naysayers have been comparing the current bull market to that of the late 1990s. Our comparison of the two shows fewer excesses in the current bull market so far. For example, the ratio of the S&P 100 to the S&P 500 remains well below its late-1990s peak

Our Understanding

The S&P 100 versus S&P 500 ratio is still nowhere near its dot-com high, so the US bull is broad, not a narrow mania waiting to snap. For India that lands through foreign flows, because a less fragile US market means a smaller chance of a violent global risk-off hitting Dalal Street.

The winners here are Indian large-cap exporters and IT shops feeding US tech budgets, plus the financials that ride sustained foreign buying. The exposed side is the frothier small and mid-cap crowd, the names most reliant on cheap liquidity that would vanish first if global breadth rolls over.

For allocators the posture is straightforward. Watch US breadth as your early warning, favour large-cap quality and export-facing technology, and stay wary of the stretched domestic small-cap end. The call inverts the moment that ratio sprints back toward its late-1990s peak.


Exencial Research Partners: http://x.com/i/article/2056936672198352896

items.title: LAST DAY: On Friday, Jerome Powell’s term as chair of the US Federal Reserve Board of Governors comes to end after months of tension between White Ho...
items.description_html:
LAST DAY: On Friday, Jerome Powell’s term as chair of the US Federal Reserve Board of Governors comes to end after months of tension between White House and @federalreserve , as US President Donald Trump pushes for more aggressive interest rate cuts.
He will be succeeded by Trump appointee Kevin Warsh, who served on central bank’s board of governors from 2006 to 2011. Powell will continue on the board as a governor after stepping down as chair.

items.title: The vote was also an important step toward making Swiggy an Indian-owned and controlled company (IOCC). As part of the proposed changes to the Article...
items.description_html:
The vote was also an important step toward making Swiggy an Indian-owned and controlled company (IOCC).

As part of the proposed changes to the Articles of Association (AoA), foreign investors SoftBank and Accel would have lost their rights to nominate board members at Swiggy.

Those board seats were set to go to the management team, giving Swiggy greater local control.

Foreign ownership in Swiggy has already come down to 52.19%, aided by the company’s Rs 10,000 crore QIP in December.

Over 80% of the QIP was subscribed to by domestic investors, including mutual funds.

However, Swiggy’s share price has fallen more than 33% since the QIP.

No wonder mutual fund investors are not happy right now.


Madhav Chanchani: 🚨 Swiggy’s management team has lost a shareholder vote to appoint CFO Rahul Bothra and co-founder Phani Kishan Addepalli to the company’s board.

The appointments will not take place now.

The proposal was part of changes to the Articles of Association (AoA) that would have

items.title: 🚨 Swiggy’s management team has lost a shareholder vote to appoint CFO Rahul Bothra and co-founder Phani Kishan Addepalli to the company’s board. ...
items.description_html:
🚨 Swiggy’s management team has lost a shareholder vote to appoint CFO Rahul Bothra and co-founder Phani Kishan Addepalli to the company’s board.

The appointments will not take place now.

The proposal was part of changes to the Articles of Association (AoA) that would have given Swiggy founder and CEO Sriharsha Majety the right to appoint a board member from the management team - in this case, Bothra.

Phani Kishan was expected to take the board seat previously held by co-founder Nandan Reddy, who left the company earlier this year.

Swiggy’s management needed 75% shareholder approval for the resolution to pass, but received only 72.36% votes in favour.

Swiggy’s remaining major pre-IPO investors (like Prosus, SoftBank, and Accel) voted in favour of the management.

However, it appears mutual funds tipped the balance against the proposal. MFs collectively own over 20% in Swiggy.

items.title: RT Deedy: The vibes in SF feel pretty frenetic right now. The divide in outcomes is the worst I've ever seen. Over the last 5yrs, a group of ~10k peop...
items.description_html:
RT Deedy
The vibes in SF feel pretty frenetic right now. The divide in outcomes is the worst I've ever seen.

Over the last 5yrs, a group of ~10k people - employees at Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, Nvidia, Meta TBD, founders - have hit retirement wealth of well above $20M (back of the envelope AI estimation).

Everyone outside that group feels like they can work their well-paying (but <$500k) job for their whole life and never get there.

Worse yet, layoffs are in full swing. Many software engineers feel like their life's skill is no longer useful. The day to day role of most jobs has changed overnight with AI.

As a result,
1. The corporate ladder looks like the wrong building to climb.
Everyone's trying to align with a new set of career "paths": should I be a founder? Is it too late to join Anthropic / OpenAI? should I get into AI? what company stock will 10x next? People are demanding higher salaries and switching jobs more and more.

2. There’s a deep malaise about work (and its future).
Why even work at all for “peanuts”? Will my job even exist in a few years? Many feel helpless. You hear the “permanent underclass” conversation a lot, esp from young people. It's hard to focus on doing good work when you think "man, if I joined Anthropic 2yrs ago, I could retire"

3. The mid to late middle managers feel paralyzed.
Many have families and don't feel like they have the energy or network to just "start a company". They don't particularly have any AI skills. They see the writing on the wall: middle management is being hollowed out in many companies.

4. The rich aren’t particularly happy either.
No one is shedding tears for them (and rightfully so). But those who have "made it" experience a profound lack of purpose too. Some have gone from <$150k to >$50M in a few years with no ramp. It flips your life plans upside down. For some, comparison is the thief of joy. For some, they escape to NYC to "live life". For others still, they start companies "just cuz", often to win status points. They never imagined that by age 30, they'd be set. I once asked a post-economic founder friend why they didn't just sell the co and they said "and do what? right now, everyone wants to talk to me. if i sell, I will only have money."

I understand that many reading this scoff at the champagne problems of the valley. Society is warped in this tech bubble. What is often well-off anywhere else in the world is bang average here.

Unlike many other places, tenure, intelligence and hard work can be loosely correlated with outcomes in the Bay. Living through a societally transformative gold rush in that environment can be paralyzing. "Am I in the right place? Should I move? Is there time still left? Am I gonna make it?" It psychologically torments many who have moved here in search of "success".

Ironically, a frequent side effect of this torment is to spin up the very products making everyone rich in hopes that you too can vibecode your path to economic enlightenment.

items.title: RT New York Magazine: Joe Lim estimates that 90 percent of what you see on the internet is advertising in disguise, and he should know. For three year...
items.description_html:
RT New York Magazine
Joe Lim estimates that 90 percent of what you see on the internet is advertising in disguise, and he should know. For three years, Lim ran a company called Floodify, which at its peak operated 65,000 dummy social-media accounts used to drum up attention on behalf of paying clients.

The point of this kind of marketing is that nobody is supposed to notice it. But lately, the machinery has started to show.

In April, Justin Bieber headlined two consecutive weekends at Coachella. Coachella is the biggest stage in pop music save only for the Super Bowl, the kind of event that in theory generates its own attention. And yet on both weekends, a Discord server writer Lane Brown had been monitoring hosted paid campaigns for Bieber’s Coachella performances, offering clippers — people who are hired to turn a song, trailer, interview, stump speech, or whatever into short, social-media-friendly fragments — as much as a dollar per thousand views.

“On social media, popular opinion is being formed, measured, and manipulated all at once, and every signal the platforms produce — a trending song, a backlash, a talking point, the feeling that ‘everybody’ is suddenly talking about the same thing — can now be fabricated by unseen actors with hidden agendas,” writes Brown.

“Everybody is doing this now,” Lim says. “And if you’re not, you’re behind.”

Brown reports on how the same techniques are now being used to fool people on every app they go to in order to find out what other people think, not just in music but across entertainment, politics, consumer products, and celebrity gossip: https://nymag.visitlink.me/w6Bu9N

items.title: Rapido’s overall round is $730 million, of which $240 million is primary capital. This implies a massive secondary sale of nearly $500 million by exi...
items.description_html:
Rapido’s overall round is $730 million, of which $240 million is primary capital.

This implies a massive secondary sale of nearly $500 million by existing investors - one of the largest private market exits over the last 18 months.

Swiggy sold 12% stake in Rapido for over $250 million.

It is likely that Rapido's early investors are also exiting, fully or partially, given that Rapido is now an 11-year-old company.


The Arc: 🚨 Rapido has raised $240 mn in funding at $3 bn valuation - a significant jump from $1.1 bn valuation in Sep 2024.

Venture firm Accel has joined as the new investor. The round was led by existing backer Prosus.

Rapido is taking on Uber in India's ride-hailing market.

items.title: RT Aditi Shrivastava: “We’ve taken money from just one Indian investor,” Anand Lunia. Early-stage investment firm India Quotient closed its fifth f...
items.description_html:
RT Aditi Shrivastava
“We’ve taken money from just one Indian investor,” Anand Lunia.

Early-stage investment firm India Quotient closed its fifth fund at $129 million in October 2025, its largest corpus to date.

The fund is over 60% bigger than its previous vehicle, which raised $80 million in 2021.

In an interview with The Arc, Lunia spoke about how the transition was executed, the gaps he sees in the seed-stage market and more.

https://www.thearcweb.com/article/india-quotient-anand-lunia-staying-small-to-own-seed-stage-dJEk2zMKlUDAN43F
